educationalContent.heading: What is a deposition summary?
paragraphs: A deposition summary condenses hours of testimony into a format you can actually work with, pulling out the most relevant information for quick reference. It captures what was said, preserves the factual backbone, and cuts the filler you'd find in a full transcript.

The Page-Line deposition summary is the most common format. It's a table: one column for a summary of each testimony section, another for the page and line citations from the transcript. Each row covers a different section, organized chronologically.

Page-Line summaries are popular because they make every key piece of testimony easy to find, give you a clear overview of what matters, and let you jump to the exact citation. The downside: they're time-consuming and labor-intensive to write by hand.

Dodonai's AI deposition summary software creates high-quality Page-Line summaries in minutes at a fraction of the cost of traditional deposition summary services. See our {{link:https://app.dodon.ai/help/generating-first-summary|step-by-step guide to generating your first summary}} or explore our {{link:https://app.dodon.ai/help/executive-summary-templates|executive summary templates}} for more formatting options. For a full walkthrough of writing deposition summaries from scratch, read our {{link:/blog/writing-winning-deposition-summaries-dodonai-comprehensive-guide/|guide to writing deposition summaries}}.

educationalContent.heading: What is deposition summary software?
paragraphs: Deposition summary software uses AI to pull the most important testimony from a deposition transcript and organize it into a usable summary.

Lawyers use deposition summaries (also called deposition digests) for litigation and trial prep, as a reference for clients and team members alongside {{link:/ai-medical-record-summaries-chronologies/|Medical Record Summaries}} and other evidentiary tools. They save attorney time and reduce costs. Before choosing a platform, see {{link:/blog/deposition-summary-software-what-reviewers-check/|what software reviewers check when evaluating deposition summary tools}}.

faq.question: What file types can Dodonai summarize?
faq.answer: Dodonai handles .pdf, .txt, .doc, .docx, .csv, and .pptx files. It can also summarize general texts, and there's a dedicated feature for {{link:/ai-medical-record-summaries-chronologies/|summarizing medical records}}.

faq.question: What file type will the deposition summary be?
faq.answer: Summaries save as .txt by default, but you can download them as .pdf or .doc. Learn more about {{link:https://app.dodon.ai/help/exporting-summaries|exporting and downloading your summaries}}.

faq.question: Is Dodonai secure?
faq.answer: Yes. Dodonai is SOC 2 certified and HIPAA compliant. We follow cloud security best practices and our LLM providers operate under zero data retention policies.

faq.question: What will you do with our data?
faq.answer: We only use your data to run the Dodonai app. Neither Dodonai nor our LLM providers will use your data to train models. Our providers operate under zero data retention, meaning nothing is stored after processing is complete.

faq.question: How much does Dodonai charge for deposition summary services?
faq.answer: The deposition summary service costs $1 per page. Your first summary is free.

faq.question: Why is a Deposition Summary valuable?
faq.answer:
Deposition transcripts can run hundreds of pages. Nobody has time to re-read all of that before a hearing. A deposition summary strips it down to what matters: key testimony, organized chronologically, with page-line citations so you can find the source fast.

Here's where they pay off:

1. Time savings: Attorneys bill by the hour. A summary that takes 3 minutes to scan beats re-reading a 200-page transcript.

2. Better trial prep: With the key testimony extracted, you can spot strengths and weaknesses quickly, plan cross-examinations, and decide which lines of questioning to push on.

3. Team alignment: Summaries give your whole team (associates, paralegals, experts) a shared reference point. Everyone works from the same facts.

4. Cost control: Reducing transcript review time means more hours for billable strategy work and lower costs for clients.

5. Witness prep: Hand a summary to an expert witness before trial and they'll know exactly what was said under oath, without reading the full transcript.

For a deeper look, read our article on {{link:/blog/6-ways-deposition-summaries-provide-value-in-litigation/|6 ways deposition summaries provide value in litigation}}.

faq.question: What is Deposition Software?
faq.answer:
Deposition software covers a range of tools that help lawyers handle depositions more efficiently. The category includes several types:

1. Deposition summary software: Automates summarizing deposition transcripts. That's what Dodonai does.

2. {{link:/ai-deposition-transcript-software/|Transcript management}}: Full-text search, bookmarking, and annotation across your transcript library.

3. Video and audio integration: Syncs multimedia files with transcript text.

4. Real-time collaboration: Shared annotations, chat, and live feeds so your team can work a deposition together.

5. Exhibit management: Categorize, tag, and link evidence to specific transcript sections.

Most firms use a combination of these tools depending on caseload and practice area. For guidance on picking the right one, see our {{link:/blog/choosing-deposition-summary-software-litigators-guide/|litigator's guide to choosing deposition summary software}}.

faq.question: What types of deposition summaries can I create?
faq.answer: Dodonai supports 3 deposition summary formats. The {{link:/blog/deposition-summary-template-page-line-format-for-motion-practice/|page-line format}} organizes testimony chronologically with page and line citations, ideal for motion practice and trial prep. {{link:/blog/how-to-draft-a-issue-based-deposition-summary/|Issue-based summaries}} group testimony by topic instead of page order, which works well for complex commercial disputes. Narrative summaries give you a flowing overview of the full testimony. You pick the format when you generate a summary.

faq.question: I have more questions. Where can I find answers?
faq.answer: Check out our {{link:https://app.dodon.ai/help/deposition-summaries|Help Center guide on deposition summaries}} for detailed instructions. You can also contact us at hello@dodon.ai with any questions.
